title: C Conditions
content:
### Conditional Statements in C

Conditional statements allow your program to make decisions based on conditions.

### Condition Types

| Condition | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `if` | Execute code if condition is true |
| `if-else` | Execute one block if true, another if false |
| `if-else if-else` | Multiple conditions to check |
| `switch` | Select one of many code blocks |
| Ternary | Short conditional expression (`? :`) |

### Truth Values

In C, any non-zero value is true, and zero is false. Conditions typically use relational or logical operators.

---

### Comparison with Other Languages

| Concept | C | Python | Java |
|---------|---|--------|------|
| True value | non-zero | `True` | `true` |
| False value | `0` | `False` | `false` |
| Boolean AND | `&&` | `and` | `&&` |
| Boolean OR | `||` | `or` | `||` |
| Boolean NOT | `!` | `not` | `!` |

---

> **Key Point:** In C, `0` is false and any non-zero value (including negative numbers) is true.

quiz.question: Which integer value represents false in C?
